added.title: Step 0 of the resumption protocol (`skills/engineering-workflow/references/resumption-protocol.md`)
added.body: new pre-Step-1 section distinguishing two resume-prompt shapes: **AI-authored handoff prompts** (dense pointer block, Steps 1â€“6 apply mechanically) vs **human-originated directives** (`continue`, `resume`, `go`, `ç¹¼çºŒ`, `resume: <verb> <object>`, or similar short user instruction). Normative rule: a short human directive is a request to act on `Manifest.next_action`, not a passive context update; `system-reminder` / MCP-state / deferred-tool availability / other runtime-injected content appearing in the same turn is **not** part of the user's directive and cannot reinterpret it into silence. If the directive is genuinely ambiguous, the incoming session declares Lazy mode, reads the Manifest, and surfaces `next_action` for the user to confirm â€” the one thing it must not do is silently end the turn. A non-mandatory `resume: <verb> <object>` user-side format is recommended for ambiguity-sensitive workflows. Cross-referenced with `docs/ai-operating-contract.md Â§11` as the symmetric outgoing-side rule â€” Â§11 governs narrate-then-act compliance on the outgoing session, Step 0 governs short-directive interpretation on the incoming session, and together they close both ends of the session boundary. Closes the "`continue` + heavy system-reminder noise â†’ `No response requested.`" silent-break failure mode reported against 1.9.0.

added.title: Manifest size ceiling sub-clause (`docs/change-manifest-spec.md` Â§State-snapshot discipline)
added.body: new sub-section after "Anti-pattern: the fat handoff prompt" naming the ~25,000 token / ~2,000 line single-file read ceiling common to AI runtimes. Rule: a manifest that crosses the ceiling stops working as a state snapshot, because the incoming session cannot open it in one read, and any fallback to `grep` / offset reads (a) bypasses the `last_updated` / `phase` / `status` header that drift-detection relies on, (b) cannot distinguish "field not populated" from "field not in this read window," and (c) produces role-divergent views of state in multi-agent changes â€” the opposite of the shared-snapshot guarantee. Prescribed remedies, in order of preference: compact in place (move verbose narrative into `implementation_notes[]` / `review_notes[]` / `handoff_narrative` / `escalations[]`) or split via `part_of` (umbrella initiative with child manifests, each independently satisfying Â§State-snapshot discipline). Handoff prompts that instruct "grep for X" or "read lines Nâ€“M" of a manifest are explicitly named as compensating for an oversized manifest â€” compact the manifest first. Closes the "oversized child manifest forcing grep/offset workaround" gap reported against 1.9.0.

added.title: Glossary entry â€” Resume prompt (`docs/glossary.md`)
added.body: new canonical term sibling to the existing `Resume mode` and `State snapshot` entries. Distinguishes the AI-authored and human-originated shapes of resume prompts, codifies the rule that runtime-injected content is not a user message, and cross-references the Step 0 normative definition. The existing `State snapshot` entry gains a paragraph on the size ceiling referencing the new sub-clause in the manifest spec.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` resumption rule
changed.body: the 3-bullet rule gains a new leading bullet ("Interpret the incoming prompt first") covering Step 0's prompt-shape distinction. The existing "Change Manifest is the state snapshot" bullet gains a clause pointing at the size ceiling, so both new rules surface in the skill-level quick reference and not only in the deep references.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/lazy-resume-checklist.md` â€” new Step 0
changed.body: pre-Step-1 section mirrors the resumption-protocol change so the 60-second checklist surfaces short-directive interpretation before mode selection. Renumbering preserved: existing Steps 1â€“5 keep their numbers.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/references/misuse-signals.md` â€” new Misuse 6
changed.body: "silently ending the turn on a short resume prompt," with signals (short directive + heavy system-reminder noise â†’ "no response requested" or empty turn) and the Step 0 fix. Complements Misuse 5 ("resuming by immediately patching"): the two together cover both over- and under-reacting to a resume prompt.

changed.title: `docs/ai-operating-contract.md Â§11 Relation to other sections`
changed.body: gains a new bullet cross-referencing Step 0 as the symmetric incoming-side rule. Â§11 governs the outgoing session; Step 0 governs the incoming session; neither alone prevents the full class of silent-break failures at role-handoff points.

changed.title: Version bump
changed.body: `plugin.json`, `marketplace.json`, `README.md` badge, and `CHANGELOG` latest-release heading all agree on `1.10.0`. Verified by the existing `version-consistency` CI job. No schema change: `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` and its JSON mirror have zero diff in this release â€” every new rule fits existing fields (`part_of`, `implementation_notes[]`, `review_notes[]`, `handoff_narrative`, `escalations[]`).

added.title: Resume-mode protocol (`skills/engineering-workflow/references/resumption-protocol.md` rewrite)
added.body: Step 2 of the resumption protocol rewritten from an unconditional "re-read at minimum" artifact dump into a **five-mode decision table** (Lazy / Targeted / Role-scoped / Full / Minimal). The incoming session's first action is now to *declare* which mode applies; only the artifacts that mode requires are read. Per-phase artifact map (Targeted) and per-role artifact map (Role-scoped) hard-coded in the protocol so the new session does not have to chase through phase files to find what to read. Full mode is preserved but **downgraded to an explicitly-declared disaster-recovery fallback** â€” every Full-mode resume must state *why* (Manifest absent / stale / drift suspected) in the first message, or it counts as a gate-discipline failure per Rule 1. A new Step 2a manifest-completeness check and Step 2b context-budget rule (~30% soft ceiling; downgrade one tier when breached) close the "I'll read everything just to be safe" reflex that was causing session-handoff context collapse on Full-mode work. Fixes the real-world failure where a 7+ surface / 50+ file Full-mode change produced a verbose handoff prompt whose sequential file-reads exhausted the incoming session's context before any implementation work began â€” both reported "session stopped responding" crashes happened in this window.

added.title: Compact handoff-prompt template (`skills/engineering-workflow/templates/handoff-prompt-template.md`)
added.body: new template for the *outgoing* session. Enforces a 400-word soft cap / 800-word hard cap on handoff prompts (measured in words, not tokens, to stay tokenizer-independent). MUST include: change_id, phase, Manifest path, resume mode + one-sentence reason, one-line next action, â‰¤ 3 open items (each pointing at a Manifest field), ROADMAP row reference, and a read block of â‰¤ 3 paths limited to what the next action genuinely needs. MUST NOT include: re-explanations of prior phases, inline copies of spec / plan / test report, re-statements of methodology rules, long file-path lists. A read block needing more than 3 paths is a Manifest-completeness failure â€” the fix is to populate the Manifest, not to expand the prompt. A Lean-mode variant (Minimal resume, â‰¤ 1 path read, Lean-spec as the snapshot) collapses into the same file. Worked Phase 3 â†’ Phase 4 example at 94 words demonstrates the budget is comfortable in practice.

added.title: Lazy-resume checklist (`skills/engineering-workflow/references/lazy-resume-checklist.md`)
added.body: new 60-second per-mode checklist for the *incoming* session. Five steps: (1) name the mode without reading anything yet; (2) apply the per-mode read list (Lazy = Manifest only; Targeted / Role-scoped = hard-coded tables; Full = disaster fallback with stated reason; Minimal = Lean-spec + current task item); (3) drift detection (six mechanical triggers: prompt over 800 words, read block over 3 paths, unresolvable Manifest path, phase mismatch, stale `last_updated`, missing listed file â€” any one stops the resume); (4) context-budget check (~30% ceiling, downgrade one tier when breached); (5) announce mode + phase + next action before any code touches. Replaces the pre-1.9.0 reflex of "re-read spec, plan, test plan, latest test report, completion report, then figure out where you left off."

added.title: State-snapshot discipline (`docs/change-manifest-spec.md` Â§State-snapshot discipline)
added.body: new normative section after "One manifest per change" that elevates the Change Manifest to its **role as the single state snapshot** for a change. Core rule: if the manifest cannot answer "what comes next" without opening another file, the manifest is incomplete â€” fix the manifest, not the reading ritual. Includes a pointer-completeness checklist enumerating which manifest fields serve as pointers for each required artifact (spec, plan, test plan, latest test report, completion report, ROADMAP row) and at which phase each becomes required. Uses **existing manifest fields only** â€” `evidence_plan[*].artifact_location`, `handoff_narrative`, `part_of` â€” so **no schema change** is required. Anti-pattern callout names the fat handoff prompt as the visible symptom of an underfilled manifest.

added.title: Glossary entries â€” Resume mode, State snapshot, Handoff extension (`docs/glossary.md`)
added.body: new canonical definitions for `Resume mode` (the five-mode system) and `State snapshot` (the Manifest's role in it). The existing `Handoff` entry gains a sentence distinguishing the handoff *prompt* (compact pointer block, capped at 400 / 800 words) from the handoff *content* (which lives in the Manifest). Cross-references into the resumption protocol, handoff template, and state-snapshot discipline ensure other docs defer to the glossary as the canonical term source per the glossary's own usage rules.

added.title: Action continuity rule (`docs/ai-operating-contract.md` Â§11)
added.body: new normative section addressing the **verbal-completion illusion**: an AI narrates "I'll now call X" and emits end-of-turn instead of the tool call. Diagnosed from real-world usage where both "session stopped responding" incidents happened at role-handoff transition points immediately after verbose narrative sentences â€” not context exhaustion, not permission denial, but the model mistaking *stating an action* for *performing it*. The rule is mechanical: when stating an action intent, the next emitted tokens must be the tool call itself, in the same turn. Risk-point inventory enumerates the five transitions where this failure concentrates (after task-list updates, after plan approval, at new phase start, after confirmation-shaped sentences, across handoff-prompt boundaries). Cross-referenced from Â§6 (stop conditions â€” Â§6 says when to stop; Â§11 says when not to), Â§9 (non-fabrication â€” Â§11 extends to fabrication by silence), and Â§7 (fact > self-narrative applied to action transitions).

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` resumption rule + misuse-protection bullet
changed.body: the 5-bullet resumption rule collapses into 3 bullets that name the discipline at the right level of abstraction: (1) declare a resume mode; (2) the Manifest is the state snapshot; (3) respect the context budget. Pointers link to the detailed protocol, checklist, and handoff template. Misuse-protection list gains a new entry naming the verbose handoff prompt as a failure mode to watch for.

changed.title: `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` downstream-agent minimum actions
changed.body: replaced the unconditional "read manifest in full + read any referenced spec / plan / test report" rule with a five-step mode-aware variant: declare resume mode first; read Manifest; read only what the chosen mode requires; check for drift; stop and report if Manifest insufficient. Most cross-role handoffs map to Role-scoped mode. The `Prohibited` list now names two symmetric failures â€” reading only the summary, *and* reading every listed artifact "to be safe" â€” and explicitly rejects handoff prompts over ~400 words as a Manifest-completeness signal.

changed.title: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` Rule 6 â€” re-entry vs resumption
changed.body: new paragraph immediately after the rule header distinguishes **re-entry** (re-opening a closed phase because new information invalidated prior work â€” Rule 6's subject) from **resumption** (reattaching context across a session break without re-opening a phase â€” the resumption-protocol's subject). A session can resume without re-entry; a re-entry always resumes afterward into the re-opened phase. Closes a confusion that otherwise lets agents conflate the two and open unnecessary ROADMAP re-entry rows on routine session boundaries.

changed.title: `skills/engineering-workflow/SKILL.md` â€” two new core operating principles
changed.body: principle 9 (batch independent tool calls in a single message; serialize only when a later call depends on an earlier one's output) and principle 10 (narration is not action; cross-references `docs/ai-operating-contract.md` Â§11). Principle 9 addresses the measurable cost of serialized tool calls in long-running stages; principle 10 surfaces the single root cause of observed role-handoff crashes in Full-mode work.

changed.title: Version bump
changed.body: `plugin.json`, `marketplace.json`, `README.md` badge, and `CHANGELOG` latest-release heading now all agree on `1.9.0`. Verified by the existing `version-consistency` CI job. No schema change: `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` and its JSON mirror have zero diff in this release â€” every new pointer requirement fits existing fields.

added.title: Role composition patterns (`reference-implementations/roles/role-composition-patterns.md`)
added.body: non-normative companion to the three-role contract, documenting four sketch patterns (Planner + research sub-agent / Planner + code-explorer sub-agent / Implementer + test-writer sub-agent / Reviewer + reference-sampler sub-agent) that let a canonical role internally decompose without violating the field-ownership contract or the anti-collusion rule. Canonical roles remain Planner / Implementer / Reviewer; sub-agents are tools the role uses, never additional roles with field authority. Four invariants are enforced: sub-agents never write manifest fields directly, sub-agents have distinct identities from every canonical role in the change, sub-agents inherit the tool-permission envelope of their parent role (cannot be granted capabilities the parent is denied), and the canonical role remains fully accountable for all output regardless of sub-agent contribution. Seven anti-patterns documented (sub-agent writes manifest fields, anti-collusion bypass via identity reuse, capability expansion, sub-agent sign-off, plausibly-complete narrative by proxy, composition in Lean/Zero modes, unbounded nesting). `reference-implementations/roles/README.md` updated with a Â§Optional: role composition pointer. Non-normative by design â€” teams may adopt zero or all of the four patterns without affecting operating-contract compliance.

added.title: Machine-readable re-entry trigger (`reference-implementations/re-entry-trigger/`)
added.body: new pure-function reference implementation of `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` Rule 6's decision table. Given two manifest states (old committed + new in-progress), `suggest_reentry(old, new)` returns the structured list of re-entry suggestions: which phase to re-open (`0_clarify` / `1_investigate` / `2_plan` / `4_implement_append` / `4_implement_evidence`), why (list of human-readable reasons), and which manifest fields must be rewritten. Six of seven Rule 6 rows covered (the "spec updated mid-change by user" row requires an external signal and is explicitly out of scope, documented in `DEVIATIONS.md`). Behaviour details: down-grades (breaking_change.level falling) do not trigger re-entry â€” only rises do, per Rule 6 literal wording; append-only suggestions are suppressed when a higher-severity re-entry also fires; SoT pattern-change detection is keyed on `info_name` identity so a rename is reported as remove+add rather than pattern-change. CLI wrapper included (`python3 suggest_reentry.py <old> <new>`) emitting JSON on stdout with exit codes 0/1/2 per the automation-contract convention. 12 unit tests covering each decision-table row plus happy path, down-grade suppression, append-only suppression, and multi-trigger cases. Not integrated into the three reference validators by default because the decision needs two manifest states; `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` Rule 6 cross-references the module so runtimes wanting mechanical re-entry prompting have a canonical implementation to embed.

added.title: Evidence tier (`schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` + Rule 2.11)
added.body: each entry in `evidence_plan` gains an optional `tier` enum `[critical, standard]`. `critical` evidence blocks Phase 6 sign-off if missing and cannot be waived into `residual_risk`; `standard` evidence may be moved to `residual_risk` with `conditional_approval`. Absent `tier` defaults to `standard` â€” pre-1.8 manifests continue to validate. The Planner assigns tier at Phase 2 Plan; the Reviewer may upgrade (triggers send-back to collect more evidence) but must not downgrade. **New automation-contract Rule 2.11** (`evidence.critical_required_for_high_severity`, blocking) enforces a floor: at least one evidence entry must be `tier: critical` when any of these conditions hold â€” breaking_change.level âˆˆ {L2, L3, L4}, rollback.overall_mode == 3, or a high-risk surface (`compliance`, `real_world`, `experience`) is touched with role=primary. All three reference validators (POSIX shell / Python / Node) implement Rule 2.11 identically, including the "backward-compat silent when no high-severity condition" and "silent when a consumer-role high-risk surface is present" cases. Two templates updated to demonstrate tier in practice: `game-gacha.yaml` (mode 3 + experience/real_world primary) and `security-sensitive.yaml` (mode 3 + compliance primary) now carry mixed `critical` / `standard` tiers; the other three templates are left without `tier` to demonstrate backward compatibility. `docs/automation-contract.md` gains a new "Â§Evidence tier" section and `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md` gains the normative pseudocode for Rule 2.11. Validator DEVIATIONS.md files updated to list Rule 2.11 as implemented across all three.

added.title: Cross-Change Knowledge Note (CCKN) pattern
added.body: new `docs/cross-change-knowledge.md` defines a lightweight artifact for reusable technical knowledge that spans multiple changes (library gotchas, third-party API quirks, platform-specific behavior, domain rules). Distinct from the per-change Change Manifest and from the temporal memory tiers in `ai-project-memory.md` â€” CCKNs are organized by **topic**, carry `topics` keyword index and `scope` tag (library / domain-rule / external-api / platform-quirk), require dated verified-references, and maintain a changelog tracking the change_ids that extended the note. Four hard limits prevent scope drift: not task-specific, not a replacement for `sot_map`, not a substitute for `evidence_plan` artifacts, stale references (>12 months) must be re-verified. Complete worked example included in the document. The manifest schema gains an optional `knowledge_notes_touched` array for reverse-lookup (given a CCKN, which changes contributed to it). `docs/ai-project-memory.md` and `docs/glossary.md` updated to cross-reference; the glossary distinguishes CCKN (topical) from Tier 2 project memory (temporal). Backward-compatible: existing manifests without `knowledge_notes_touched` remain valid; the new field is optional.

added.title: Optional machine-readable pre-filter (`docs/multi-agent-handoff.md`)
added.body: a new section between the single-agent anti-collusion rule and the manifest progression phases defines an optional sub-agent layer that runs between the Implementer's handoff and the Reviewer's audit. Purpose: cheap binary structural verification (does every `artifact_location` resolve, does every cited identifier exist via code-search, are `surfaces_touched` and diff consistent) so the Reviewer spends audit time on substance rather than structural gaps. Four hard limits prevent drift into the Reviewer's role: (1) not a Reviewer replacement â€” passing pre-filter only proves structural completeness; (2) Reviewer citing "pre-filter passed" as basis for approval triggers Anti-Rationalization Rule 4; (3) pre-filter outputs binary findings only, never subjective scores; (4) disabled in Zero-ceremony and Lean modes (Full mode only). Pre-filter identity must differ from Planner / Implementer / Reviewer under the same anti-collusion rule as the canonical roles. `docs/ai-operating-contract.md` Â§10 updated with a cross-reference clarifying the pre-filter is **adjacent to**, not a substitute for, the AI's own six-question self-check.

added.title: Operational cheat sheet (`docs/operational-cheat-sheet.md`)
added.body: a single-page, skim-optimized lookup surface added to bridge the gap between "don't know where to start" and "read the normative doc in full." Structure: per-role top 5 actions (Planner / Implementer / Reviewer, each with deep links to the source doc), five-second checks for the standard change vetting questions (surface / SoT / breaking change / rollback / evidence), and a "when you see X, go to Y" navigation table covering the common situations (schema change, public-API consumer, mobile long-lived client, payment/push/on-chain, plan gap mid-implementation, multi-agent handoff, etc.). Entry points (README.md "Reading paths" + orientation.md header + AGENTS.md "Recommended reading order") updated to reference it as the 30-second lookup path. Explicitly not a rule spec â€” rules remain in the normative docs â€” and explicitly calls out "if this sheet feels like overhead, the task probably belongs in the skip bucket" so adding the cheat sheet does not become new mandatory ceremony.

added.title: Reading-order guide in `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md`
added.body: a new section after the TL;DR that clarifies three pairs of rules that look redundant to first-time readers but are not: (1) Global self-check (ai-operating-contract Â§10, 6 questions, for every AI delivery) vs Pre-handoff self-check (agents/implementer.md, 5 questions, Implementer-specific before advancing `phase: review`); (2) Phase re-entry (phase-gate-discipline Rule 6, internal workflow bookkeeping) vs Breaking-change migration path (breaking-change-framework Paths A/B/C, external consumer handling â€” can apply simultaneously to one change); (3) Anti-rationalization 6 rules (mechanical heuristic) vs "Rubber-stamp is not a finding" (principle statement â€” rules refine the principle, do not replace it). Closes the "am I being asked to do the same thing twice?" reader confusion that surfaced during Stage 1 review.

added.title: Scenario coverage limitations in `docs/onboarding/when-not-to-use-this.md`
added.body: a new section after "Situations where the methodology does not apply" listing eight scenarios where the methodology **partially fits but has known gaps**: incident response under pressure, pure research / exploratory work, A/B test / experiment design, pure content / i18n / copy-only changes, AI agent / prompt system's own development, UI/UX design iteration, ML model training / hyperparameter iteration, open-source library's own development. Each row names why the fit is imperfect and gives a pragmatic path today (backfill Lean manifest as post-mortem; use CCKN for research findings; run design-spike outside the methodology then Lean on the implementation phase; etc.). The section distinguishes itself from the preceding "methodology does not apply" table: those situations skip the methodology entirely, these scenarios use parts of it and document the gap honestly. Closes the "this doesn't fit my situation, so I'll ignore the whole thing" failure mode.

added.title: Implementer pre-handoff self-check (five questions)
added.body: `agents/implementer.md` and `reference-implementations/roles/implementer.md` gain a role-specific self-check that must clear before advancing `phase: review`. The five questions (acceptance-criterion coverage with concrete `file:line`; verification coverage per criterion; reference-existence verified via code-search; pattern alignment with `sot_map`; evidence-path completion) enforce honest reporting at the Implementer â†’ Reviewer boundary. Vague / hedged answers are failing answers â€” the Implementer returns to the work or triggers the Discovery loop. Global self-check in `docs/ai-operating-contract.md` Â§10 still applies; this sits on top of it. Scales with ceremony: Lean keeps all five; Zero-ceremony collapses to one question.

added.title: Reference-existence verification protocol (`docs/ai-operating-contract.md` Â§2a)
added.body: new section naming the *protocol* the AI uses to avoid fabricating identifiers. Section 9 ("Non-fabrication list") is the prohibition; Â§2a is the act. Source-tree identifiers require a code-search producing a concrete `path:line`; external references require a network-fetch confirming the URL resolves and the referenced anchor exists, with verification date cached; in-flight artifact references require a file-read confirming presence. Failure to verify downgrades the claim to an `assumption` and triggers the Discovery loop. The Reviewer has an explicit **sampling right** (documented in `agents/reviewer.md` and `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md`) to pick any cited identifier and require the Implementer to reproduce the verification command plus output on request.

added.title: Reviewer anti-rationalization rules (`agents/reviewer.md` + `reference-implementations/roles/reviewer.md`)
added.body: six hard send-back triggers that catch rubber-stamping in language even when the Reviewer is mechanically prevented from editing code: (1) perfect-confidence hallucination, (2) hedging language, (3) unsubstantiated `pass` entries, (4) read-only review without any verification command executed, (5) editing through the back door via prose-dictated patches, (6) thin residual-risk section. Any one applying is a mandatory send-back, not a judgment call. `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` updated to cross-reference the rules and the sampling right in the Reviewer section.

added.title: Phase-gate-discipline Rule 5a â€” Final artifacts are produced from a working space
added.body: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` gains a sub-rule under Rule 5 that requires phase-boundary artifacts (manifest fragments, ROADMAP rows, `review_notes`, `handoff_narrative`, completion reports) to be drafted in a session-scoped, auditable, non-canonical working space before being copied into their canonical location. Prevents thinking-in-progress from polluting canonical artifacts and gives the Reviewer an audit surface per anti-rationalization rule 1. Scales with ceremony: Zero-ceremony waives the rule entirely; Lean applies once (before Implementer codes); Full applies at every boundary. Runtime-specific working-space locations are documented in `docs/bridges/*`, not here.

added.title: Phase-gate-discipline Rule 6 â€” Phase re-entry protocol
added.body: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` gains a sixth rule with a decision table mapping variation types (new surface touched / SoT mis-classified / breaking-change level rises / rollback mode rises / implementation strategy changes / evidence insufficient / spec updated mid-change) to the exact re-entry phase, the manifest fields that must be rewritten, and the commit-history treatment. Every re-entry opens a fresh ROADMAP row with an explicit `phase_reentry` marker; silent re-entry is prohibited. Re-entry frequency per initiative becomes a usable anti-metric per Principle 10. `agents/reviewer.md` send-back instructions and `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/fix-retest-loop.md` both cross-reference the rule so the right re-entry phase is chosen mechanically rather than by intuition.

changed.title: Phase-gate-discipline heading "The five rules" â†’ "The six rules"
changed.body: reflects the addition of Rule 6 (phase re-entry protocol). Rule 5a is an extension of Rule 5 and is not counted separately. Existing references to Rules 1â€“5 remain stable.

changed.title: `agents/implementer.md`, `reference-implementations/roles/implementer.md`, `skills/engineering-workflow/phases/phase4-implement.md`
changed.body: Discovery loop and Handoff sections updated to reference Rule 6 (phase re-entry) and the pre-handoff self-check. No change to existing field-ownership semantics or tool-permission matrix.

changed.title: `agents/reviewer.md`, `reference-implementations/roles/reviewer.md`, `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md`
changed.body: Reviewer sections augmented with anti-rationalization rules, reference-existence sampling right, and a pointer to Rule 6 for choosing the correct send-back re-entry phase. No change to the absolute "no write / edit tools" constraint, which remains the single most important enforcement in the multi-agent contract.

fixed.title: Claude Code plugin layout non-conformance
fixed.body: `agents/` and `hooks/` were placed under `.claude-plugin/`, which violates the Claude Code plugin convention that requires components at plugin root and only `plugin.json` inside `.claude-plugin/` (per https://code.claude.com/docs/en/plugins.md). The Customize panel in Claude Desktop silently listed only the Skills tab for this plugin; Agents and Hooks never surfaced because the autoloader does not scan `.claude-plugin/agents/` or `.claude-plugin/hooks/`. Moved the three sub-agent definitions from `.claude-plugin/agents/` to `agents/` (via `git mv` so history is preserved). Pre-existed from 1.6.0 when the role artifacts were introduced; surfaced now because the discoverability gap was not visible until the Claude Desktop Customize panel was inspected directly.

added.title: `hooks/hooks.json` plugin entry point
added.body: a thin wrapper that makes the existing Claude Code hook bundle discoverable as plugin hooks. Wires the five reference hooks (`manifest-required`, `evidence-artifact-exists`, `consumer-registry-check`, `sot-drift-check`, `completion-audit`) onto `PreToolUse` / `PostToolUse` / `Stop` events using `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}` paths. Scripts continue to live under `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/hooks/` so that the Cursor / Gemini CLI / Windsurf / Codex bridges can reference the same source â€” no duplication. Users can toggle individual hooks on or off in the Customize panel.

changed.title: Cross-reference sweep for the new plugin layout
changed.body: `README.md` layout tree updated so `agents/` and `hooks/` are shown at plugin root with `.claude-plugin/` reduced to the two manifest files; README auto-load sentence now names all four autoload surfaces (`plugin.json`, `skills/*/SKILL.md`, `agents/*.md`, `hooks/hooks.json`) with an inline reminder that components live at plugin root, not under `.claude-plugin/`; `AGENTS.md Â§7`, `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` Â§Enforcement across runtimes, and `reference-implementations/roles/README.md` all updated from `.claude-plugin/agents/{...}` to `agents/{...}`. Historical references under `CHANGELOG.md` / `CHANGELOG.json` / `ROADMAP.md` closed-initiative entries intentionally left as-is per the repo's "CHANGELOG is a factual record, do not rewrite history" discipline (CLAUDE.md Â§3).

fixed.title: `consumer-registry-check` hook yq query
fixed.body: the recursive-descent filter `.. | .external_registry_url? // empty` uses jq syntax that mikefarah/yq v4 rejects (`lexer: invalid input text "empty"`). The hook swallowed the yq error via `2>/dev/null`, leaving `urls` empty so every invocation silently exited 0 â€” the `warn-unreachable` selftest fixture never triggered a warning, but the failure was masked because the repo's CI had not yet run the selftest on a machine with yq installed. Replaced with the yq-v4-idiomatic filter `.. | select(has("external_registry_url")) | .external_registry_url`. Pre-existed from 1.3.0; surfaced on the first post-push CI run, which is how CI earns its keep.

fixed.title: `validator-node` test script glob
fixed.body: the `test` script invoked `node --test 'dist/tests/**/*.test.js'`; the single-quoted pattern is opaque to bash (no expansion) and Node 20's `--test` does not parse glob patterns as paths, so the CI job failed with `Could not find '.../dist/tests/**/*.test.js'`. Replaced with an explicit file path (`node --test dist/tests/rules.test.js`) which Node 20+ handles directly. Pre-existed from 1.7.0 Sprint 4 P6; same "first real CI run exposes it" dynamic as above.

fixed.title: `hooks-claude-code` selftest now skips `yq`-dependent cases cleanly on local dev machines without `yq`
fixed.body: previously `selftest.sh` printed a warning and then ran every fixture anyway, producing 9 FAILs across the four yq-dependent hook directories (`completion-audit`, `consumer-registry-check`, `evidence-artifact-exists`, `sot-drift-check`) because the hooks correctly degrade to `exit=2` (TOOL_ERROR) but fixtures assert `exit=0` or `exit=1`. The adapter selftests (cursor / gemini-cli / windsurf / codex) already solved the same problem with an `# SKIP yq not on PATH` marker; this patch ports the pattern. Summary line now reports `N cases, F failed, K skipped` when any case skipped. CI (`.github/workflows/validate.yml` â†’ `hooks-selftest`) is unaffected â€” it installs `yq` explicitly, so every case executes on every push / PR exactly as before.

changed.title: `hooks-claude-code/selftests/README.md` drift fixes
changed.body: header corrected from "four hooks" to "five hooks" (leftover drift from 1.3.0's `consumer-registry-check` addition); Requirements section rewritten to describe the new SKIP-on-missing-yq pattern; Minimum-coverage table extended to 14 cases with the previously-omitted `consumer-registry-check.sh` row.

added.title: Dual-format schemas (YAML authoritative + generated JSON mirror)
added.body: every `schemas/*.yaml` now has a machine-consumable `schemas/*.json` sibling generated by `.github/scripts/generate-schema-json.py`. YAML remains the single source of truth (authoring + review); JSON is a derived artifact for tooling that prefers or requires JSON Schema. A new `schema-json-drift` CI job regenerates all JSON mirrors and fails if any diverges from the committed copy, so the two representations cannot drift silently. Rationale: editors and validators across the Node / browser / JVM ecosystems often consume JSON Schema directly without a YAML step; publishing both lets consumer projects validate manifests without adding a YAML loader to their toolchain.

added.title: Machine-readable CHANGELOG (`CHANGELOG.json`)
added.body: generated by `.github/scripts/generate-changelog-json.py` from the Markdown source so the Markdown stays the writing surface while downstream tooling (release dashboards, dependency bots, methodology-drift checkers) consumes the structured form. Schema: `releases[] { version, date, sections { Added?, Changed?, Removed?, Deprecated?, Fixed?, Security? } }` with each section a list of bullet-prose strings. The `version-consistency` CI job now parses `CHANGELOG.json` via `python3 -c` rather than grepping the Markdown, so the structured release heading is load-bearing.

added.title: Schema deprecation markers (`$defs.deprecation`)
added.body: `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` gains a reusable `$defs.deprecation` object with `since`, `removal_at`, `replacement`, `rationale` fields. Applied via `anyOf` on fields where L3 (source-of-truth shape) or L4 (public contract) breaking changes require a deprecation window â€” this aligns the schema with automation-contract-algorithm rule 2.6 (every L3 / L4 breaking change MUST carry a deprecation timeline with a removal date). Existing manifests remain valid because `deprecation_timeline` is retained as an alias; the rule layer in `validator-python` and the new `validator-node` accept either field name for backwards compatibility.

added.title: Onboarding path consolidation
added.body: `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` is now the single normative entry point for new readers; competing pointers from README, AGENTS.md, and the bridge index all funnel through it. The page gained a top-of-file "What you read next" decision tree keyed by role (new contributor / consumer-project integrator / bridge author / validator implementer / auditor), pointers to the runtime-hook-contract and the validator trio, and a Â§Contract + automation section rewritten to reflect the dual-format schemas and the validator-python / validator-node / validator-posix-shell split. Prior scattered onboarding bullets across README / AGENTS.md were shortened to one-liners that link here.

added.title: Language-native Node validator (`validator-node`)
added.body: `reference-implementations/validator-node/` is a TypeScript + Node 20 reference that implements every layer of `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md` with parity against `validator-python`. Built with plain `tsc` targeting ES2022 / NodeNext; tests run via Node 20's built-in `node --test` against compiled JS (no `tsx` / `ts-node` dependency). Runtime deps limited to `ajv` (with `{strict: false, logger: false}` to avoid the `ajv-formats` dependency â€” rule 1.3 already enforces ISO-8601 at the rule layer, so format-assertion loss has no coverage impact), `glob`, and `yaml`. 13 tests under `tests/rules.test.ts` exercise fixtures copied byte-for-byte from the Python reference so any rule-layer divergence surfaces as a parity bug. CI `.github/workflows/validate.yml` gains a `validator-node-tests` job running `npm ci && npm test` on every push / PR. `DEVIATIONS.md` documents the `ajv-formats` omission and the path-resolution quirk (fixtures live at `tests/fixtures/`, compile output lives at `dist/tests/`, so tests resolve fixtures via `../../tests/fixtures`). The three validators â€” POSIX-shell reference (minimal, portable), Python (feature-complete), Node/TypeScript (feature-complete, ecosystem-native) â€” give consumer projects a validator in whichever language their CI already speaks.

added.title: Runtime-hooks-in-practice how-to
added.body: `docs/runtime-hooks-in-practice.md` is a practical companion to the normative `docs/runtime-hook-contract.md`: installation per runtime (Claude Code / Cursor / Gemini CLI / Windsurf / Codex), how to read a hook-refusal message and what to fix, how to author a new category-C (drift) hook that matches the exit-code contract, and troubleshooting for the common failure modes (manifest not found, `yq` missing, network-dependent hook without timeout, settings-file event-name drift). The contract document now has a visible home for "I am wiring this up right now" content without polluting the normative spec.

changed.title: Top-level README slimmed from ~400 to ~296 lines
changed.body: the "Agent-runtime hooks" section was a 100-line feature-by-feature enumeration that duplicated `docs/runtime-hook-contract.md` and `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/README.md`; replaced with a ~15-line summary that defines the primitive, lists the five reference hooks by category, and funnels readers to the normative contract + the new how-to. The "What you get" block and the "When your situation matches" block both gained validator-trio and deprecation-discipline entries so the slimmed content still surfaces the new capabilities, just via shorter prose.

changed.title: Cross-reference sweep across entry-point docs
changed.body: `README.md` layout tree lists the new JSON schema mirrors, `CHANGELOG.json`, and `validator-node`; `AGENTS.md` Â§Structured output enumerates all four schema files (canonical YAML + generated JSON mirrors) and Â§Reference implementations lists both validator-python and validator-node; `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` Navigation map adds the runtime-hook-contract pointer and the validator-trio bullet, and Â§Contract + automation is rewritten; `docs/diagrams.md` Â§6 "See also" now references the canonical + JSON-mirror schema pair and enumerates the three validator references. No orphan links; every new artifact is discoverable from at least one of README, AGENTS.md, and orientation.md.

changed.title: Version bump
changed.body: `plugin.json`, `marketplace.json`, `README.md` badge, and `CHANGELOG` latest-release heading now all agree on `1.7.0`. Verified by the existing `version-consistency` CI job, which this release extends to read `CHANGELOG.json` instead of grepping Markdown. Closes the 4-sprint post-1.3.0 improvement programme (machine-readability + onboarding-clarity initiative).

added.title: Non-Claude-Code multi-agent enforcement
added.body: Planner / Implementer / Reviewer role separation is now published for runtimes that cannot gate tool exposure per persona. `reference-implementations/roles/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.md` ship the runtime-neutral role prompts (paste-ready for Cursor Custom Mode, Gemini CLI session, Windsurf mode, Codex profile). `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` adds a Â§Enforcement across runtimes matrix distinguishing mechanical enforcement (Claude Code per-agent `tools:` frontmatter; Cursor Custom Mode excluding edit/apply tools) from prose-only enforcement (Gemini CLI / Windsurf / Codex â€” paired with distinct sessions per role, OS-level read-only working dir for Reviewer, and session-identity recording in `approvals` to keep the anti-collusion rule auditable). `AGENTS.md Â§7` tightened with the per-runtime bullet list; `.windsurfrules`, `GEMINI.md`, and the Cursor `.cursor/rules/{planner,implementer,reviewer}.mdc` bridges updated so every non-Claude-Code entry point points at the role artifacts.

added.title: Cross-runtime adapter selftests
added.body: `reference-implementations/hooks-{cursor,gemini-cli,windsurf,codex}/selftests/selftest.sh` ship hermetic TAP-ish smoke tests per adapter. Each selftest runs the runtime adapter's `parse-event.sh` inside a clean subshell with synthetic runtime env vars (CURSOR_HOOK_TRIGGER/CURSOR_TOOL, GEMINI_HOOK_EVENT/GEMINI_TOOL_NAME, WINDSURF_HOOK_EVENT/WINDSURF_TOOL_NAME, CODEX_HOOK_EVENT/CODEX_TOOL_NAME) and asserts `AP_EVENT` / `AP_TOOL` / `AP_STAGED_FILES` / `AP_PHASE` come out correct. Four cases per adapter: unset-env-yields-empty-ap-vars, runtime-env-maps-to-event-and-tool, manifest-yaml-populates-phase (skipped if `yq` absent), missing-manifest-leaves-phase-empty. CI `.github/workflows/validate.yml` now runs all four adapter selftests on every push / PR. Each adapter's `DEVIATIONS.md Â§"No runtime-level selftest"` downgraded from gap to covered-but-narrow: **adapter layer** is now tested; **runtime-wiring layer** (Cursor actually invoking the adapter with the documented env var names) remains an unavoidable per-workspace smoke test because it depends on the runtime's own event wiring.

added.title: Adoption anti-metrics doc
added.body: `docs/adoption-anti-metrics.md` is a **non-normative** diagnostic aid for catching the specific failure mode where a team adopts the methodology's ceremony without adopting its substance. Names seven anti-signals (evidence paths all pointing at the same artifact; `rollback.overall_mode` always 1; `surfaces_touched` always 4/4 or always 1/4; review notes always `LGTM`; phase-gate ticks land faster than commits; manifests copy-and-renamed across changes; `cross_cutting.*` always boilerplate) paired with counter-signals, "why this happens" root-cause paragraphs so readers fix the incentive not the symptom, and non-normative detection sketches. Opens with an explicit warning that operationalizing these as CI gates would create Goodhart targets â€” they are for review conversations, not gates. `docs/automation-contract.md` and `docs/operational-disciplines.md` cross-reference the new doc; `AGENTS.md` reading order lists it after `docs/adoption-strategy.md`.

added.title: Security-sensitive worked example
added.body: `templates/change-manifest.example-security-sensitive.yaml` demonstrates the full manifest surface for a JWT signing-key rotation: exercises the `compliance` extension surface alongside the four canonical surfaces; SoT pattern 8 (Dual-Representation) for signing keys with a required `dual_representation` object capturing editor / runtime / sync-step / CI sync-check; pattern 4 for the claims contract; pattern 1 for the audit event schema; `cross_cutting.security` with `impact=high`, `pii_access_added=false` (claims unchanged), `supply_chain_review_needed=true` (verifier-library behavior is the trusted dependency); `breaking_change.level=L1` escalated to L2 structural because long-tail consumers (JWKS cache TTL, mobile app install base) cannot refresh synchronously; `rollback.overall_mode=3` with a compensation_plan because issued tokens cannot be recalled; mixed `per_surface_modes` showing that a single change can be mode-1 on ops config, mode-2 on server deploy, and mode-3 on the public contract; evidence plan citing SAST sarif, threat-model diff, dual-kid integration test, rotation-runbook dry-run, compliance changelog; two resolved `escalations` (`security_or_pii_path` + `rollback_mode_3`). Validates against `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` via the existing `template-conformance` CI job.

changed.title: Index hygiene for Sprint 3 additions
changed.body: `README.md` "When your situation matches" block adds three new discovery paths (multi-agent roles on non-Claude-Code runtime â†’ enforcement matrix + role artifacts; security / supply-chain / PII path â†’ appended security-sensitive example; adoption-review path â†’ anti-metrics doc). `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` Navigation map adds bullets for the anti-metrics doc and the `reference-implementations/roles/` directory, appends a worked-example pointer on the security-supply-chain line, and adds an enforcement-across-runtimes callout on the multi-agent-handoff line. `AGENTS.md` reading order lists the anti-metrics doc after adoption-strategy; Structured-output section includes the security-sensitive example; Reference-implementations section includes the roles directory. All additions grep-clean: no orphan link targets.

added.title: iOS/Swift stack bridge + surface map
added.body: `docs/bridges/ios-swift-stack-bridge.md` maps the four canonical surfaces to a Swift 5.9+ / iOS 15+ UIKit + SwiftUI mixed codebase. Covers UIKit â†” SwiftUI interop as four boundary patterns (UIHostingController, UIViewControllerRepresentable, NotificationCenter bridges, shared `@Observable` view model); multi-process state via App Groups + Widget + Share Extension + Darwin notifications as Pattern 4a + Pattern 8 instances; CloudKit rollback asymmetry (public DB forward-only mode 3 vs private DB effectively mode 2); Core Data `.xcdatamodeld` lightweight vs heavyweight migration as a three-way dual representation (bundle â†” generated classes â†” on-device store); App Store compliance surface singling out PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy, Info.plist usage descriptions, AASA, `.entitlements`, and StoreKit catalog as review-gated files; build-time risk (WMO, Swift 6 strict concurrency, DEBUG-only code, App Thinning) and uncontrolled-external register (iOS major, App Store review, StoreKit policy, network reachability variance). Machine-consumable companion `docs/bridges/ios-swift-surface-map.yaml` validates against `schemas/surface-map.schema.yaml` and unblocks validator rule 3.2 for iOS repos.

added.title: React / Next.js stack bridge + surface map
added.body: `docs/bridges/react-nextjs-stack-bridge.md` covers React 18+ / Next.js 14+ App Router. Pipeline order documented as `middleware â†’ RSC render â†’ route handlers â†’ client hydration â†’ server actions` (Pattern 4a); the four-layer Next.js cache model (Data Cache / Full Route Cache / Router Cache / Browser-CDN) with per-layer invalidator responsibility makes explicit why `revalidateTag` alone is not always sufficient; dual-representation hotspots (DB schema â†” generated TS types, Zod schema â†” `z.infer` type, server HTML â†” hydrated DOM) as Pattern 8 instances; runtime-export (`edge` vs `nodejs`) as a per-route capability contract; rollback modes (client-component change = mode 2, middleware matcher change = mode 3, Server Action shape change = mode 3, nullable-column additive DB migration = mode 2). Pages Router, pure SPA React, and React Native explicitly out of scope with pointers to project-local overlay docs; experimental flags (`ppr`, `dynamicIO`) pinned to project-local addenda. Machine-consumable companion `docs/bridges/react-nextjs-surface-map.yaml` validates against the same schema.

added.title: iOS/Swift worked example
added.body: `docs/examples/ios-swift-app-example.md` walks through adding a CloudKit-synced tag feature with a Home Screen Widget end-to-end: private CloudKit schema (mode 2), public CloudKit schema (mode 3, staged to next release), Core Data v3 â†’ v4 lightweight migration tested against a 10k-row store, App Group snapshot struct owned in a shared Swift package to prevent Widget â†” app drift, PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy updates tied to the iCloud data-type change, and Widget AppIntent parameter stability as a user-configuration contract. Phase 0â€“7 structure identical to other bridge-linked examples.

added.title: React / Next.js worked example
added.body: `docs/examples/react-nextjs-app-example.md` walks through migrating a Pages Router form to App Router with a Server Action. Exercises belt-and-suspenders `revalidateTag` + `revalidatePath` for Data Cache + Full Route Cache invalidation, middleware pipeline order (A/B assignment must run **after** auth or the bucket flips on sign-in), Server Action shape change as Pattern 4 mode 3 with one-release legacy compat, Prisma schema as the upstream dual-representation SoT (Pattern 8) with CI `prisma generate` diff gate, and Client Component promotion as a performance-budget surface change (bundle analyzer diff in the PR is the evidence).

added.title: Bridge/example index updates
added.body: `README.md` stack-bridges list, `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` both bridge-enumeration blocks, and `AGENTS.md` worked-examples + stack-bridges lists now include iOS/Swift and React/Next.js so both additions are reachable from every top-level discovery point.

added.title: Self-validation CI workflow
added.body: `.github/workflows/validate.yml` runs five jobs on every push / PR to `main`: (1) **schema-syntax** validates every `schemas/*.yaml` against the JSON Schema 2020-12 meta-schema; (2) **template-conformance** validates every `templates/change-manifest.example-*.yaml` AND `examples/starter-repo/change-manifest.yaml` against `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml`; (3) **hooks-selftest** runs the hermetic hook bundle selftest with `yq` installed; (4) **validator-python-tests** runs the Python reference validator's pytest suite; (5) **version-consistency** confirms `plugin.json` / `marketplace.json` / README badge / CHANGELOG latest release agree on the same version. Supporting scripts: `.github/scripts/validate-schema-syntax.py`, `.github/scripts/validate-templates.py`, `.github/scripts/check-version-consistency.sh`. The workflow uses **no untrusted event inputs** â€” every `run:` command invokes a hardcoded script or fixed tool invocation, eliminating injection surface. The repo now holds itself to the same evidence-before-completion contract it preaches.

added.title: Runnable starter-repo example
added.body: `examples/starter-repo/` is a minimal, self-contained end-to-end demonstration: a schema-valid `change-manifest.yaml` for a tiny change (adding a `/healthz` endpoint), three cited evidence artifacts under `evidence/`, a closed ROADMAP initiative, a 30-line `scripts/validate-manifest.py` using only `pyyaml` + `jsonschema` (no dependency on this repo's packages), and a `Makefile` with `install` / `validate` / `clean` targets. Running `make install && make validate` in a fresh clone produces `ok   change-manifest.yaml` in under a minute, showing the full contract â€” surface mapping, SoT map with Host-Lifecycle pattern 10 for the uptime counter, rollback mode 1, evidence plan with three collected artifacts, residual-risk acceptance, multi-author provenance â€” executing against the real schema. Intended as both onboarding material and a seed for consumer projects. README "What you get" adds bullet 7 pointing at it.

added.title: All-pieces-together architectural diagram
added.body: `docs/diagrams.md` Â§6 adds a four-layer Mermaid stack map showing how the **normative layer** (`AGENTS.md` + `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` + `docs/runtime-hook-contract.md` + `docs/ci-cd-integration-hooks.md`), **execution layer** (the engineering-workflow skill + Phase 0â€“8 gates), **carrier-artifact layer** (`change-manifest.yaml` + `evidence/*` + `ROADMAP.md`), and **mechanical-enforcement layer** (runtime hooks + CI hooks) plug together. Dashed arrows show the feedback direction â€” guards refuse, they do not edit. README "What you get" and `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` one-paragraph-version both add a top-of-page callout linking into the diagram so readers get the full stack in one scroll before drilling into per-doc detail.

added.title: Repo-level contribution hygiene
added.body: `.github/SECURITY.md` (threat surface + in-scope / out-of-scope lists + dual-path private reporting preferring GitHub security advisory with email fallback + 30-day disclosure timeline + safe-harbor statement), `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/{bug,doc,bridge}.md` (three purpose-specific templates with YAML frontmatter â€” bugs for broken scripts / schemas / hooks, doc for cross-doc drift and methodology gaps, bridge for proposing new stack bridges), `.github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/config.yml` (security-advisory contact link + blank-issue escape hatch), `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md` (Lean / Full / Trivial scope classification + 4-surface checklist + SoT impact + scriptable evidence checkboxes + breaking-change level + rollback mode + cross-cutting concerns + ROADMAP-initiative link). Templates mirror the methodology's own vocabulary so a PR to this repo exercises the framework the repo defines.

changed.title: README version badge realigned
changed.body: badge read `1.0.0` through 1.1.0 â†’ 1.3.0 despite the rest of the manifest set advancing; pre-existing drift detected by the new `check-version-consistency.sh` script on its first run. Bumped to `1.4.0` along with this release. The script now enforces consistency across `plugin.json`, `marketplace.json`, the README badge, and the latest CHANGELOG release heading on every CI run, so this drift cannot reappear silently.

changed.title: Template drift fixes uncovered by new CI
changed.body: `templates/change-manifest.example-mobile-offline.yaml` had `host_type: android_process` which is not a valid enum value for `host_lifecycle.host_type`; corrected to `mobile_os_process`. `templates/change-manifest.example-multi-agent-handoff.yaml` was using pre-1.0 field names (`info`, `sot_path`, string-form `pattern`, `mode/notes` under rollback) and cross-document YAML aliases that PyYAML does not resolve across `safe_load_all()` documents; rewritten end-to-end against the current schema with each snapshot self-contained. Both drifts were caught by the new `template-conformance` CI job on its first run â€” the self-validation foundation validating itself.

changed.title: Diagrams page TL;DR realigned
changed.body: `docs/diagrams.md` intro previously enumerated six phantom diagrams (SoT pattern taxonomy / breaking-change decision / rollback-mode selection / agent-handoff) against a file containing a different set of five (surfaces / phase flow / Lean-Full decision / SoT relationships / team adoption). Rewrote the TL;DR to match the actual content plus the new Â§6 stack map.

added.title: Per-bridge surface-map artifacts
added.body: `docs/bridges/flutter-surface-map.yaml`, `docs/bridges/android-kotlin-surface-map.yaml`, `docs/bridges/android-compose-surface-map.yaml`, `docs/bridges/ktor-surface-map.yaml`, `docs/bridges/unity-surface-map.yaml` publish each bridge's mapping from the four canonical surfaces (plus stack extensions such as asset / performance-budget / compliance / experience / data-quality) to machine-consumable file-path globs. The new `schemas/surface-map.schema.yaml` (JSON Schema 2020-12) validates the shape; the artifacts unblock validator rule 3.2 (surface â†” file-pattern drift) in `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md`.

added.title: Hook bundle self-test harness
added.body: `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/selftests/` ships a hermetic, framework-free fixture runner (`selftest.sh`, `run-case.sh`, a `git` stub, and eleven fixtures covering all four category-A / B / C / D hooks). The harness PATH-shims `git`, `cd`s into each case directory, and matches against a minimal `expected` grammar (`exit=`, `stderr~=`, `stdout~=`). Closes the `DEVIATIONS.md Â§1` gap; CI jobs that enable these hooks in `block` mode should now run the selftest on every PR that touches `hooks/` or `selftests/`.

added.title: Network-degradation reference hook
added.body: `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/hooks/consumer-registry-check.sh` demonstrates the runtime-hook-contract clause that "checks requiring network must degrade to advisory." The hook probes every `.consumers[].external_registry_url` in the manifest with `curl --max-time ${AGENT_PROTOCOL_NET_TIMEOUT:-5}` and warns with `exit 2` on timeout, DNS failure, non-HTTP URL, or non-2xx response â€” never blocks. A `curl` stub + three fixtures (`pass-no-consumers`, `pass-reachable`, `warn-unreachable`) cover the branches hermetically. `settings.example.json` adds a `PreToolUse` + `Bash(git push*)` matcher wiring it at pre-push. Closes `DEVIATIONS.md Â§4`.

added.title: Cross-runtime hook adapters
added.body: thin adapter bundles at `reference-implementations/hooks-cursor/`, `reference-implementations/hooks-gemini-cli/`, `reference-implementations/hooks-windsurf/`, and `reference-implementations/hooks-codex/`. Each ships a runtime-native settings example (Cursor / Windsurf / Codex use JSON, Gemini CLI uses TOML), a README, a DEVIATIONS.md documenting the contract-trigger â†’ native-event mapping, and an `adapter/parse-event.sh` that normalizes the runtime's event payload into cross-runtime `AP_EVENT` / `AP_TOOL` / `AP_STAGED_FILES` / `AP_PHASE` env vars. Hook scripts are not duplicated; each adapter's settings point back at the Claude Code bundle's `hooks/` directory so a regression fix propagates everywhere with a single edit. `README.md` hook section and `docs/runtime-hook-contract.md` "Relationship to other documents" updated to list the four new adapters; `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/DEVIATIONS.md Â§5` downgraded from "only Claude Code covered" to "cross-runtime coverage â€” partial" with wiring smoke tests listed as the remaining gap.

added.title: Language-native validator
added.body: `reference-implementations/validator-python/` is a Python 3.10+ reference that implements every layer of `docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md`, including the four rules the POSIX reference defers: 2.4 (decomposition graph acyclicity via DFS), 2.5 (`depends_on` â†” `blocks` bidirectional mirror via sibling manifest scan), 3.2 (surface â†” file-path drift via `--surface-map docs/bridges/<stack>-surface-map.yaml`), and 3.4 (uncontrolled-interface monitoring-channel staleness via a local `.agent-protocol/monitoring-cache.json`). Exit codes (0 / 1 / 2) stay identical to the POSIX reference. Shipped with a 12-test pytest suite covering every closed-gap rule. `validator-posix-shell/DEVIATIONS.md` now points the four unsupported rows at this validator as the recommended implementation; root `README.md` layout tree updated to list both.

added.title: Project-local overlay pattern
added.body: `docs/bridges-local-deviations-template.md` is the canonical section skeleton (Parent bridge, Overlay name + purpose, Surface mapping, SoT mapping, Rollback mapping, Drift signals, Anti-patterns, Example stubs, Review + retirement, See also) that consumer projects copy into their own repo as `docs/bridges-local-deviations.md`. `docs/bridges-local-deviations-howto.md` walks through authoring a Flame (Flutter game engine) overlay end-to-end â€” the worked example shows how a small-audience or stack-adjacent overlay (Flame, Unity UaaL, KMM-iOS depth, visual-scripting graphs, event-sourcing, vendor-specific APM exporter, iOS Swift sibling bridge) stays project-local rather than bloating the mainstream bridge index. Each of the five upstream bridges' "Known limitations" sections now links to the template + how-to so the local-overlay path is discoverable from the bridge the consumer is already reading.

changed.title: Orientation entry point clarified
changed.body: `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` is the canonical single-page onboarding doc; README, AGENTS.md, CHANGELOG, and ROADMAP references updated to match. Five-rules + navigation + walkthrough content unchanged.

changed.title: Language policy tightened
changed.body: in `README.md`, the `[1.0.0]` Design invariants block, and the orientation page â€” forward-looking, single-sentence statement without enforcement-mechanism elaboration.

changed.title: Phase-gate discipline examples retargeted
changed.body: `docs/phase-gate-discipline.md` now uses an enum-split migration and a JSON-Schema validation command as its illustrative gate examples.

fixed.title: Claude Code hook wiring
fixed.body: `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/settings.example.json` used invented event names (`preCommit` / `postToolUse` / `stop`) that Claude Code silently ignores, so hooks never fired. Rewrote the file to use the actual native events (`PreToolUse` / `PostToolUse` / `Stop`, PascalCase) with the correct matcher-grouped structure. `pre-commit` semantics now map to `PreToolUse` with `matcher: "Bash(git commit*)"` â€” this fires **before** the Bash tool runs `git commit`, so exit code 1 actually cancels the commit (the 1.2.0 `postToolUse` mapping would have fired after the commit, giving the hook no blocking power). `docs/runtime-hook-contract.md` registration example updated to match. `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/DEVIATIONS.md` notes the historical mismatch.

added.title: Agent-runtime hooks section in `README.md`
added.body: new top-level section covering: what runtime hooks are (vs. CI hooks), the four reference hooks + their checks + block/warn levels, Claude Code install steps (copy the bundle + merge `settings.example.json`), contract-trigger-to-native-event mapping table, configuration environment variables (`AGENT_PROTOCOL_MANIFEST_PATH` / `MIN_EVIDENCE_PER_PRIMARY` / `LEAN_SKIP_MANIFEST` / `STRUCTURED_OUTPUT`), an adoption-ramp recommendation (weeks 1-4, observe â†’ gate â†’ evidence â†’ completion), and guidance on writing custom hooks. Bullet 7 added to the "What you get" section at the top of the README.

added.title: Correct install-path guidance
added.body: in `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/README.md` â€” the "What's here" table now shows both the abstract contract trigger and the concrete Claude Code event + matcher for each of the four hooks; a new note explains why `PreToolUse` (not `PostToolUse`) is the right choice for commit-gating hooks.

added.title: Multi-agent role separation â€” Claude Code bridge
added.body: `.claude-plugin/agents/planner.md`, `.claude-plugin/agents/implementer.md`, `.claude-plugin/agents/reviewer.md` ship the three canonical Full-mode roles as Claude Code sub-agent definitions. Each definition encodes the role's responsibilities plus its tool-permission envelope: Planner has sub-agent delegation (`Task`) but no edit/write capability; Implementer has edit/write but no sub-agent delegation (cannot spawn reviewers of itself); Reviewer intentionally lacks edit/write entirely so a single AI identity cannot both implement and approve its own change. `docs/multi-agent-handoff.md` adds two normative sections â€” a capability-category tool-permission matrix and a single-agent anti-collusion rule (Lean-mode exception permits Planner â‰¡ Implementer; Implementer â‰¡ Reviewer is forbidden outright). `AGENTS.md` Â§7 carries the tool-permission matrix + anti-collusion rule so the constraint is runtime-neutral; `GEMINI.md`, `.windsurfrules`, and `.cursor/rules/engineering-workflow.mdc` add per-runtime summaries pointing at the canonical doc.

added.title: Runtime hook contract + reference bundle
added.body: `docs/runtime-hook-contract.md` defines the complementary of `docs/ci-cd-integration-hooks.md`: how an agent runtime (not CI) consumes manifest events. Four categories (A phase-gate / B evidence / C drift / D completion-audit), JSON-over-stdin event schema, exit-code semantics 0 / 1 / 2 identical to the automation contract, latency budgets (< 500 ms phase-gate & evidence; < 2 s drift), and non-functional requirements (offline, deterministic, no side effects, no model-in-hook). `reference-implementations/hooks-claude-code/` provides an executable bundle: four POSIX-shell hooks (`manifest-required.sh`, `evidence-artifact-exists.sh`, `sot-drift-check.sh`, `completion-audit.sh`), a `settings.example.json` showing Claude Code event wiring with `agentProtocol.category` metadata, a README with install paths + environment variables, and a DEVIATIONS.md. Hooks parse the manifest via `yq` and emit contract-compliant exit codes; bundle is portable to any runtime via its own event-registration mechanism. `docs/ci-cd-integration-hooks.md` gains a "Relationship to runtime-layer hooks" section; `docs/automation-contract.md` cross-refs the runtime contract.

added.title: `strategic_parent` schema field
added.body: `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` adds an optional top-level object anchoring a manifest to an external strategic artifact (ADR / RFC / OKR / design doc / external ticket). Required fields `kind` + `location`; optional `summary` (â‰¤ 400 chars) + `initiative_id` (stable cross-manifest identifier for aggregation queries). `docs/strategic-artifacts.md` (new) explains what the anchor is and what it deliberately is NOT (no ADR / RFC format definition, no "Phase -1" ceremony, no parent-content validation), the `part_of` vs `strategic_parent` relationship (internal-epic vs external-decision â€” complementary), and anti-patterns (stale pointers, PR-URL back-fills, trivial Lean-mode over-use). `docs/change-manifest-spec.md` adds a `strategic_parent` subsection to the Decomposition-relationship fields section.

added.title: Per-stack worked examples
added.body: `docs/examples/flutter-app-example.md` (multi-platform "Save & Share" exercising platform-channel two-sided contract and per-target rollback asymmetry), `docs/examples/android-kotlin-example.md` (offline draft-saving exercising Room migration + WorkManager unique-name discipline + ViewBinding dual-representation), `docs/examples/ktor-server-example.md` (order-lifecycle enum + migration + plugin install order + three-rollback-mode discipline within one feature), `docs/examples/unity-game-example.md` (save-data format migration exercising schema versioning + IL2CPP / AOT build-time risk + three-track rollback asymmetry). Each bridge's Reference Worked Example section now points at its dedicated file; the Ktor and Android-Kotlin bridges' "future update" placeholders are removed.

added.title: Jetpack Compose stack bridge
added.body: `docs/bridges/android-compose-stack-bridge.md`. Companion to the Kotlin + XML bridge: inherits Room / WorkManager / FCM / runtime-permission / vendor-fork / deep-link / ProGuard-R8 sections and adds Compose-specific deltas for state ownership (`remember` / `rememberSaveable` / ViewModel + `StateFlow`), recomposition hygiene, effect-API pipeline order (`LaunchedEffect` / `SideEffect` / `DisposableEffect`) as a new Pattern 4a case, `CompositionLocal` provider nesting as a Pattern 4a case, navigation-route strings as a Pattern 3 enum, Compose compiler metrics as a drift signal, and Paparazzi / Roborazzi + `createComposeRule()` verification. XML bridge's "known limitations" entry for Compose now cross-references this bridge.

added.title: Flutter bridge deduction-closure sections
added.body: `docs/bridges/flutter-stack-bridge.md` now covers: Flutter Web target discipline (`dart2js` / `dart2wasm` pipelines, service-worker cache invalidation, CDN-flag rollback vs. store binaries), Flutter Desktop target discipline (window lifecycle, per-OS packaging, signing / notarization asymmetry), federated plugin lifecycle (app-facing / platform-interface / per-platform-impl trio with version-compat tables), code push / dynamic delivery (Shorebird / custom OTA as Pattern 4a + store-policy constraints), and state-management library pipeline-order (Provider / Riverpod / Bloc initialization order as Pattern 4a).

added.title: Android Kotlin bridge deduction-closure sections
added.body: `docs/bridges/android-kotlin-stack-bridge.md` now covers: KMM shared-code SoT (`expect` / `actual` declarations as Pattern 4, per-target builds, serialization drift), Play Feature Delivery (install-time / conditional / on-demand / instant-app module rollback characteristics + SoT-split fan-out), and instrumented-test OS-API version discipline (silent-skip anti-pattern, min / mid / max API matrix enforcement).

added.title: Ktor bridge deduction-closure sections
added.body: `docs/bridges/ktor-stack-bridge.md` now covers: observability depth discipline (logs / metrics / traces each as Pattern 4 with cardinality budgets, coroutine-context propagation of `trace_id`, PII hashing), multi-tenant / sharding overlay (row-level / schema-per-tenant / DB-per-tenant rollback asymmetry with both default and per-tenant auth-provider pipeline-order variants, cross-tenant drift as a unique failure mode), gRPC parallel IDL overlay (protobuf-specific L0â€“L4 + dual-IDL manifest fields), GraphQL parallel IDL overlay (SDL as Pattern 4, DataLoader registration as Pattern 4a, GraphQL-specific L0â€“L4 with no wire-level versioning, subscription lifecycle as Pattern 10, federation / stitching as cross-subgraph contract, N+1 + query-complexity budget), hexagonal / onion / clean architecture overlay (remapping SoT source fields to domain ports), virtual threads (Loom) / alternative concurrency overlay (pinning hazards + reactive-framework substitution), and a JVM / Kotlin / Ktor / coroutines version-drift discipline for the build-time-risk manifest field.

added.title: Unity bridge deduction-closure sections
added.body: `docs/bridges/unity-stack-bridge.md` now covers: DOTS / ECS overlay (API stability caveat scoped to companion subpackages since core `Entities` is GA 1.x; system update-order graph as canonical Pattern 4a, three-way authoring â†” baked â†” archetype dual-representation, Burst + Baker + IL2CPP triple verification), render pipeline variant discipline (URP / HDRP / built-in RP as pipeline-specific asset targets, shader-variant-count budget, pipeline migration as its own change type), and custom engine fork discipline (fork as an additional SoT layer, patch-set contract with upstream, `UPSTREAM.md` requirement, fork-exit-plan expectation). Scope note updated so these are first-class overlays, not deferrals.

changed.body: `README.md`, `AGENTS.md`, and `docs/onboarding/orientation.md` bridge indexes updated to include the Compose bridge.
`AGENTS.md` worked-examples index updated to include the four new per-stack examples.
`docs/stack-bridge-template.md` TL;DR bridge list updated to include the Compose bridge.

Each of the four base bridges' "Known limitations" lists rewritten to reflect only residual items after the deduction-closure sections â€” prior entries that are now covered in-bridge (Flutter web/desktop/federated/code-push/state-mgmt; Android KMM/PFD/instrumented-test API gap; Ktor multi-tenant/sharding/gRPC/hexagonal/Loom; Unity DOTS/ECS/render-pipelines/custom-engine-fork) have been removed or retargeted to narrower residual scopes.

fixed.body: Android Kotlin bridge Scope note no longer calls the Compose bridge "(future)" â€” it is now linked as an existing companion with an inherited-sections summary.

Unity DOTS / ECS overlay caveat clarified â€” core `Entities` package is GA 1.x (since 2023) with stable `IComponentData` / `ISystem` / `[UpdateInGroup]`; the volatility is scoped to companion subpackages (Entities Graphics, DOTS Animation, Unity Physics for ECS, Netcode for Entities).

Ktor multi-tenant pipeline-order overlay adds the per-tenant auth-provider variant (Keycloak realm-per-tenant, Auth0 organization, per-tenant OIDC issuer, regulated B2B) where `TenantResolution` correctly precedes `Authentication` â€” the previous guidance presented the single-auth-provider order as universal.

notes.title: Operating contract
notes.body: `AGENTS.md` defines the universal runtime contract: honest reporting, scope discipline, source-of-truth rules, surface-first analysis, evidence requirements, Change Manifest contract, phase-gate discipline.

notes.title: Engineering workflow skill
notes.body: `skills/engineering-workflow/` provides Lean and Full modes, phase minimums for Phase 0 through Phase 8, capability-category guidance, and spec / plan / test / completion templates. Supporting references cover the mode decision tree, discovery loop, resumption protocol, startup checklist, and misuse signals.

notes.title: Methodology documentation
notes.body: `docs/` contains the four canonical surfaces plus nine extension surfaces (asset, experience, performance-budget, data-quality, compliance, hardware-interface, real-world, model, uncontrollable-external), ten Source-of-Truth patterns, the L0â€“L4 breaking-change severity matrix, three rollback modes (Reversible / Forward-fix / Compensation-only), cross-cutting concerns, the AI operating contract, security and supply-chain disciplines, change decomposition, team / org-scale disciplines, AI project memory, multi-agent handoff, automation contract, and phase-gate discipline.

notes.title: Change Manifest contract
notes.body: `schemas/change-manifest.schema.yaml` plus four worked example manifests (CRUD, mobile offline, game gacha, multi-agent handoff progression). The schema enforces human-approver waivers, expiry timestamps, bidirectional decomposition links, and phase-appropriate narrative fields.

notes.title: Stack bridges
notes.body: `docs/bridges/` maps the tool-agnostic methodology to concrete stacks: Flutter, Android Kotlin + XML, Ktor, Unity 3D. Bridge files are the only place where specific vendor / framework / language names appear. The stack-bridge template (`docs/stack-bridge-template.md`) supports adding your own stack.

notes.title: Automation contract
notes.body: capability-level specification (`docs/automation-contract.md`) plus normative algorithm (`docs/automation-contract-algorithm.md`) defining three forced check layers (structural / cross-reference / drift), exit-code stability, and offline operability. A non-normative reference validator (`reference-implementations/validator-posix-shell/`) built on POSIX shell plus `yq` plus a pluggable schema validator illustrates the contract.

notes.title: Worked examples
notes.body: `docs/examples/` covers ten domains: worked (batch-resend invoices), bugfix (pagination-filter desync), refactor (order-status canonical alignment), migration-rollout (structured-preferences SoT transition), game-dev (in-game shop), game-liveops (three-track asset/config/binary strategy), mobile-offline-feature, ml-model-training, data-pipeline, embedded-firmware (OTA with hardware-interface + real-world surfaces).

notes.title: Multi-agent entry points
notes.body: packaged simultaneously as a Claude Code plugin (`.claude-plugin/`), a Cursor rules bundle (`.cursor/rules/`), a Gemini CLI instruction set (`GEMINI.md`), Windsurf rules (`.windsurfrules`), and an AGENTS.md-compatible contract for Codex / Aider / OpenCode.

notes.title: English-only.
notes.body: All normative content â€” methodology, skills, schemas, templates, operating contract, READMEs â€” is authored in English.

notes.title: Tool-agnostic.
notes.body: Specific vendor, model, framework, or product names appear only in `docs/bridges/` and `reference-implementations/`, both of which are explicitly marked non-normative. Methodology docs, the skill layer, the schema, and the Change Manifest templates remain stack-neutral.

notes.title: Capability categories, not tool names.
notes.body: Every operational instruction names a category (file read, file write, code search, shell execution, sub-agent delegation) rather than a specific tool, so the plugin ports cleanly between AI runtimes.

notes.title: Canonical terminology held fixed.
notes.body: Severity L0â€“L4 (Additive / Behavioral / Structural / Removal / Semantic-reversal), rollback modes 1/2/3 (Reversible / Forward-fix / Compensation-only), four canonical surfaces plus extension surfaces, SoT patterns addressed by number, automation tiers L0â€“L3, three multi-agent roles by responsibility (Planner / Implementer / Reviewer).
